checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked the live effect-creation flow in `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:297-310`; it generates effects through the `ai-generate-effect` edge function, whose prompt contract and parameter schema types live in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts:3-20,53-75`. The plan only calls out `src/tools/video-editor/effects/effectPromptTemplate.ts`, so it does not fully address the repository's actual AI-generation path for documenting `useAudioReactive()` or `audio-binding`.

Checked the current effect wrapping path in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:108-184` and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/entrances.tsx:3-9`. The plan is aligned with the explicit requirement to leave `wrapWithEffect` and `wrapWithClipEffects` unchanged and to keep audio delivery hook-based instead of adding audio props.

Checked preview integration in `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:141-176,485-495`. The Remotion tree is created inside `makePreviewComposition()` and then passed as `component={PreviewComposition}` to `<Player>`, so the provider has to be inserted inside the returned composition component; wrapping the outer Player element would not satisfy the requirement.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked the parameter types and defaults in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:13-23`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx:16-48`, and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts:5-11`. All three currently hard-code `number | string | boolean`, so adding an `audio-binding` object value requires widening those signatures; the plan mentions new cases but does not explicitly fix the existing annotations that would reject `{ source, min, max }` defaults and display values.

Checked `validateAndCoerceParams()` in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts:22-31`. The final `else` branch is color validation, so any new parameter type not handled before that branch gets silently coerced to the color fallback; `audio-binding` therefore needs an explicit branch before the color fallback or object values will be discarded at runtime.

Checked the proposed offline-analysis mechanism against the current repository and Remotion render path. There is no existing FFT helper in the repo, `OfflineAudioContext` plus `AnalyserNode` is not enough for deterministic per-frame offline extraction, `ResolvedTimelineConfig.output` has no duration field in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx:100-162` / `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:30-38`, and the repo has no `delayRender`/`continueRender` handling, so the provider design is underspecified for correct preview/export behavior.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Searched for parameter-schema handling across the video editor and edge generator. Beyond the frontend files named in the plan, `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts:3-20,61-75` duplicates `ParameterType` and `ParameterDefinition` and teaches the model which parameter types are valid, so the underlying change is broader than the listed frontend scope.

Checked where parameter defaults are consumed with `rg getDefaultValues`. `EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:199-201,324-379`, `ClipPanel.tsx:76-93,261-408,443-456`, and `BulkClipPanel.tsx:86` all depend on the same default-value behavior, so adding `audio-binding` affects creation, editing, and application paths across the feature rather than only `ParameterControls.tsx`.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: Checked all prompt-template locations. The repository has both `src/tools/video-editor/effects/effectPromptTemplate.ts` and the production generator prompt in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts`; the plan only names the first file, so it misses supporting infrastructure that actually drives `EffectCreatorPanel` generation.

Checked preview-call plumbing in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:436-440` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:182-190`. The panel currently receives only `previewAssetSrc` and `timelineFps`, not audio clips or timeline config, so any non-synthetic preview strategy based on real timeline audio needs additional caller and prop changes that the plan does not list.

Checked supporting automated infrastructure around the generator. The edge function already has tests in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/index.test.ts`, so if the prompt contract or parameter schema changes, that test surface is part of the touched locations even though it is not listed in the plan.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: Checked every caller of `wrapWithEffect()` in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:124-184` and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:35-48`. All callers pass `params` through as raw `Record<string, unknown>`, so an `audio-binding` parameter will still arrive inside effect code as an object unless the plan also defines a helper or convention for resolving that object against `useAudioReactive()`; the current plan leaves that runtime contract underspecified.

Checked compile-effect callers in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:3,43-59`. Both sync and async registrations ultimately compile through `compileEffect` and `compileEffectAsync`, so adding `useAudioReactive` in `compileEffect.tsx` is sufficient for the runtime-injection part and does not require additional registry call-site changes.

Checked `EffectCreatorPanel` callers in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:436-440`. The caller arguments are limited to visual asset preview and FPS, which means the plan's optional actual-audio preview idea is not supported by current call sites and would need broader API changes than the plan states.

checks.question: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?

findings.detail: Checked existing composition metadata patterns in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:30-38` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx:31-44`. This codebase already centralizes timeline-duration math in helpers and computes Remotion metadata outside `TimelineRenderer`, so introducing a custom `totalDurationInFrames` prop sourced from `config.output` would cut across the prevailing pattern instead of reusing the shared helper or `useVideoConfig()`.

Checked comparable provider and hook usage in the repo, including `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.tsx` and `src/shared/contexts/IncomingTasksContext.tsx`. A dedicated provider plus a `useAudioReactive()` hook fits the general context-based approach used elsewhere for cross-cutting state.

Checked fallback handling and found near-duplicate `getFallbackValue()` implementations in `src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx:16-33` and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts:5-12`. Extending both separately will work, but it continues an existing duplication point rather than matching a more centralized maintainable pattern.

checks.question: Is there convincing verification for the change?

findings.detail: Checked existing tests with `rg ParameterControls|getDefaultValues` and `rg EffectCreatorPanel` under `src/**/*test*`; there are currently no direct tests for `ParameterControls`, `getDefaultValues`, or `EffectCreatorPanel`. The plan's proposed verification therefore needs new UI-level coverage for those pieces rather than relying mainly on `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx`.

Checked the live AI-generation surface and its tests. Because `EffectCreatorPanel` calls the `ai-generate-effect` edge function, convincing verification also needs edge tests around `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts` or `index.test.ts` to prove the new global and parameter type are exposed to the model; the current test plan does not mention that path.

Checked render-path readiness and found no current Remotion render-delay handling in the repo. If the implementation does asynchronous audio fetching and decoding inside the composition, verification should include a render-path check that rendering waits for analysis readiness; otherwise unit tests can pass while export playback still renders silence.

checks.question: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?

findings.detail: Checked the listed `must` criteria against the planned verification. `EffectCreatorPanel preview works for audio-reactive effects` and `All 11 entrance, 6 exit, and 5 continuous built-in effects are unaffected` are reasonable goals, but as written they are not yet review-verifiable from the proposed automated checks and still depend on broad manual judgment unless the plan adds explicit automated assertions for preview behavior and built-in-effect coverage.

Checked the compile/regression criteria against existing tests in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx`. The `must` goal that non-audio custom effects continue to compile and run identically is appropriately prioritized because this test file already exercises the main compile and wrapping surface.

Checked the lower-priority criteria list. `No new Remotion packages added` and `AudioAnalysisProvider new file is under ~300 lines` are correctly non-must goals because they are implementation preferences rather than product-correctness gates.

flags.concern: video-editor-effect-generation: The plan updates a legacy prompt template but misses the live edge-generator prompt and parameter-schema definitions used by `EffectCreatorPanel`.
flags.evidence: Effect creation goes through `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:297-310`, while the active generator contract lives in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts:3-20,53-75`.

flags.concern: video-editor-audio-analysis: The proposed offline-analysis design is technically underspecified because `OfflineAudioContext` rendering does not give deterministic per-frame FFT data through `AnalyserNode` as written.
flags.evidence: The plan describes `OfflineAudioContext` plus `AnalyserNode`, but the repository has no existing FFT helper and would need manual chunked analysis of the rendered audio buffer.

flags.concern: video-editor-render-pipeline: Async audio fetching and decoding would race Remotion preview and export because the current composition path has no render-delay handling.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx` is rendered directly by the Player and web renderer, and `rg delayRender|continueRender|cancelRender src` returns no matches.

flags.concern: video-editor-parameter-schema: `audio-binding` needs object-valued defaults and display and validation support in multiple duplicated type definitions, not just a new union member.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:6-23`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx:16-48`, `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts:5-31`, and `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts:3-20` all currently assume scalar defaults.

flags.concern: video-editor-effect-params: The plan adds `audio-binding` as a second runtime contract, but current callers still pass `params` through unchanged and the plan does not define how bindings become usable effect behavior.
flags.evidence: `wrapWithEffect()` in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:124-132` and `EffectLayerSequence.tsx:35-48` pass raw param objects directly into effects with no binding-resolution step.
